title: Sauropus glaucus

This family consists of a wide variety of flowering plants, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. intro: Sauropus glaucus, also known as Peruvian pepper or Lakshmi vithai, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. Traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, Sauropus glaucus is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antidiabetic properties. The bioactive constituents of Sauropus glaucus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Sauropus glaucus is commonly prepared as a decoction, powder, or capsule, and is often used to treat various health conditions, including diabetes, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues.
